source_jobs.title: Head of Public Policy
source_jobs.description:
Paxos is looking for a Head of Public Policy to manage the development of our policy positions and how we engage with the public, policymakers and regulators on these topics. We engage with national and global leaders on multiple topics relating to the future of finance, the digital economy, capital markets, blockchain and cryptocurrencies, and this role will be critical to ensuring that Paxos is at the forefront of these conversations. Ideal candidates will have deep, hands-on experience and understanding of the legislative process in the US, strong bipartisan contacts amongst policymakers, regulators and agencies, and a passion for clearly defining and articulating well-constructed policy. 

About Paxos

Paxos is on a mission to enable the movement of any asset, any time, in a trustworthy way. Today’s financial infrastructure is archaic, expensive, inefficient and risky - supporting a system that leaves out more people than it lets in. So we’re rebuilding it. 

As a regulated blockchain infrastructure company, we use technology to tokenize, custody, trade, and settle assets for enterprise clients like PayPal, Novi, Bank of America, and Interactive Brokers. Paxos is a top-funded blockchain company, with more than $500 million in total funding from leading investors like OakHC/FT, Founders Fund, PayPal Ventures, and Declaration Partners. Together, we empower today’s financial leaders to build a more open, trusted economy. 

As our Head of Public Policy, you'll get to:

 - Public Policy - Develop Paxos’ position on relevant public policy matters; strategize and execute on engagement with policymakers nationally and internationally. 

 - Industry Collaboration - Serve as Paxos representative to relevant industry associations and coalitions; engage the industry collaboratively to push forward meaningful initiatives and ideas.

 - Regulatory Affairs - Help oversee completion of regulatory applications, productively engage with current and potential future regulators.

 - Policy Communications - Work collaboratively with the Communications team to amplify Paxos policy perspectives, and to ensure that policymakers are regularly updated with relevant Paxos developments. 

 - Content - Write white papers, thought pieces, contributed pieces, blog posts and other materials on policy.

 - Cross-functional Collaboration - Work closely with senior executives to ensure that all policy work is aligned with the overall business strategy and goals. 

Your experience should include:

 - 7+ years of experience working with senior policymakers, regulators and agencies

 - In-the-weeds expertise in finance and fintech policy issues

 - The ability to work with cross-functional executives in a fast-paced environment

 - The ability to learn new concepts quickly and communicate them clearly both verbally and in written form

 - An approach that includes strategic thinking as well as excellent execution

source_jobs.title: Senior Manager of Total Rewards & People Operations
source_jobs.description:
Paxos is on a mission to enable the movement of any asset, any time, in a trustworthy way. Today’s financial infrastructure is archaic, expensive, inefficient and risky - supporting a system that leaves out more people than it lets in. So we’re rebuilding it. 

As a regulated blockchain infrastructure company, we use technology to tokenize, custody, trade, and settle assets for enterprise clients like Mastercard, Visa, Robinhood, and PayPal. Paxos is a top-funded blockchain company, with more than $500 million in total funding from leading investors like OakHC/FT, Founders Fund, PayPal Ventures, and Declaration Partners. Together, we empower today’s financial leaders to build a more open, trusted economy.

We’re looking for a highly analytical, hands-on leader to join our People team as the Senior Manager of Total Rewards and People Operations. In this role, you’ll own global total rewards, including compensation, equity, benefits, job leveling, and board/compensation committee deliverables while also overseeing the design and execution of key People programs. This is a unique opportunity to blend strategic impact with tactical execution, shaping competitive, scalable frameworks while rolling up your sleeves to get things done in a fast-paced, high-growth environment.

As a Manager (Sr. Manager) of Total Rewards and People Operations, you’ll get to:

Total Rewards

 - Lead the design and management of global compensation, benefits, and equity programs, ensuring competitiveness, fairness, and compliance.

 - Own compensation committee deliverables, including preparing board decks and presenting insights to senior leadership.

 - Design and maintain job architecture, leveling frameworks, and compensation bands to support scale.

 - Manage annual compensation cycles (merit, bonus, equity refresh) in partnership with Finance and leadership.

 - Provide thought partnership to executives and managers on compensation philosophy and pay decisions.

People Operations & Programs

 - Oversee the design and rollout of people programs including performance management, engagement surveys, onboarding, compliance, and systems/vendor renewals.

 - Partner with HRBPs, Talent Acquisition, and L&D to ensure processes and programs are consistent, efficient, and aligned with business priorities.

 - Lead HR systems improvements, automation, and reporting to increase accuracy and scalability.

 - Monitor people metrics and build advanced dashboards to provide actionable insights to executives and the Board.

 - Ensure policies, processes, and systems remain compliant across multiple geographies.

Your experience should include:

 - 12-15 years of progressive HR/People experience, with at least 10-12 years focused on compensation, total rewards and People Operations.

 - Proven experience supporting and scaling organizations through high-growth environments, ideally in technology or regulated industries.

 - Strong expertise in global compensation, benefits, equity, and job architecture, with experience managing compensation cycles and board/committee deliverables.

 - Exceptional analytical skills are required -  advanced ability to model, interpret, and present complex data, especially in compensation analysis, to drive clear, strategic decisions.

 - Hands-on experience with HR systems (Workday preferred) and proven ability to leverage technology for reporting, analytics, and process improvements.

 - Experience overseeing and implementing a wide range of HR programs (performance management, engagement, onboarding, compliance, etc.).

 - A track record of thriving in very fast-moving, ambiguous environments with sound judgment and proactive problem-solving.

 - Designing and quickly implementing solutions that cut across multiple disciplines.

 - Successful management of competing priorities, taking initiative, and driving projects to completion in a timely and accurate manner.

 - Strong communication and influencing skills across all levels, fro

source_jobs.title: Accounting Manager
source_jobs.description:
About Paxos 

Today’s financial infrastructure is archaic, expensive, inefficient and risky — supporting a system that leaves out more people than it lets in. So we’re rebuilding it.

We’re on a mission to open the world’s financial system to everyone by enabling the instant movement of any asset, any time, in a trustworthy way. For over a decade, we’ve built blockchain infrastructure that tokenizes, custodies, trades and settles assets for the world’s leading financial institutions, like Mastercard, Visa, Robinhood, and PayPal. 

The Finance Team is looking for an Accounting Manager to join our rapidly growing FinTech Company.  The Accounting Manager will lead the Company’s month-end closing process. The ideal candidate is process driven, technically sound and a great team player and coach. 

As an Accounting Manager, you’ll get to:

 - Lead the monthly, quarterly and financial closing process

 - Manage and lead members of our growing accounting team

 - Supervise the daily accounting operations

 - Work with the internal team to successfully execute the external audits 

 - Ensure compliance with GAAP and international statutory reporting requirements

 - Review reconciliations, work papers and supporting schedules to ensure they are prepared with high quality

 - Guide the team in the development, implementation and maintenance of accounting policies and internal controls

 - Aid in the development and implementation of new or enhanced, scalable processes to support the company’s growth, and drive continuous improvement

 - Enable the Company to meet internal and external reporting requirements

 - Immerse yourself in blockchain technology, cryptocurrency for enterprises and investors, our regulation-first approach, and more!

Your experience should include:

 - A minimum of 6 years of experience with a combination of public accounting (audit) and private / in-house accounting. 

 - Start-up and/or financial services experience

 - Bachelor’s degree in accounting, CPA license and strong knowledge of GAAP

 - Experience overseeing and managing an accounting team.

 - Proficiency with NetSuite ERP or other cloud based accounting software

 - Drive to continuously improve the accounting operations, controls and financial reporting processes including leverage our systems and technologies to improve workflows and scalability

 - Excellent organizational, time management, communication and interpersonal skills

Important Notice for Paxos Applicants

We’ve become aware of fraudulent accounts posting as Paxos recruiters on LinkedIn and other platforms. These scammers attempt to deceive applicants into paying for job opportunities or providing personal financial information. 

To verify a legitimate Paxos recruiter: 

 - We only use @paxos.com email addresses

 - We never ask for payment or financial details to apply, interview, or work here

 - For technical roles, we do not perform a coding interview without prior screening by our engineering team 

Thanks for your interest in Paxos!

source_jobs.title: Senior Accountant, Financial Close
source_jobs.description:
About Paxos

Today’s financial infrastructure is archaic, expensive, inefficient and risky — supporting a system that leaves out more people than it lets in. So we’re rebuilding it.

We’re on a mission to open the world’s financial system to everyone by enabling the instant movement of any asset, any time, in a trustworthy way. For over a decade, we’ve built blockchain infrastructure that tokenizes, custodies, trades and settles assets for the world’s leading financial institutions, like Mastercard, Visa, Robinhood, and PayPal.

About the team

The accounting team provides in-depth guidance to the rest of the organization on financial results, delivering essential insights that influence key decisions—from daily operations to long-term strategies. The team is responsible for solving complex financial questions in an evolving environment with new regulations, technologies and financial challenges. Their efforts help Paxos drive innovation, expand market reach and ultimately achieve Paxos’ mission of building a more open and inclusive financial system.

About the role

As a  Senior Accountant on the Accounting team, you’ll play a critical role in helping the team with the month-end, quarterly and financial closing process. You’ll have the opportunity to dive into new accounting standards and drive their implementation. You’ll be essential in helping with Paxos’ day-to-day accounting activities, overseeing aspects of the general ledger including but not limited to, cash management and intercompany functions. You'll collaborate with cross-functional teams, including strategic finance, treasury and operations to provide valuable insight. This role is critical to Paxos’  success, ensuring that all reporting timelines are met and financial information is accurate, enabling informed decisions that drive growth and profitability.

What you'll do 

 - Perform both routine and non-routine/complex monthly close responsibilities

 - Analyze books and records on a monthly basis to ensure accuracy and completeness

 - Ensure compliance with GAAP and international statutory reporting requirements

 - Liaise with external audit firm on both routine and non routine matters

 - Participate in and guide the the development, implementation and maintenance of accounting policies and internal controls

 - Assist in designing new processes and enhance existing ones; implement scalable processes to support the Paxos’ growth, and drive continuous improvement

 - Enable Paxos to meet internal and external reporting requirements

 - Immerse yourself in blockchain technology, cryptocurrency for enterprises and investors, our regulation-first approach, and more!

About you

 - A minimum of 3 years of combined experience in public accounting (audit) and private/in-house accounting 

 - Experience in start-ups and/or financial services 

 - Bachelor’s degree in accounting, CPA license and strong knowledge of GAAP

 - Experience overseeing and managing accounting staff

 - Proficiency with NetSuite ERP or other cloud-based accounting software

 - Strong drive to continuously improve the accounting operations, controls and financial reporting processes, including leveraging systems and technologies to improve workflows and scalability

 - Excellent organizational, time management, communication and interpersonal skills

source_jobs.title: Controller
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT PAXOS

Today’s financial infrastructure is archaic, expensive, inefficient and risky — supporting a system that leaves out more people than it lets in. So we’re rebuilding it.

We’re on a mission to open the world’s financial system to everyone by enabling the instant movement of any asset, any time, in a trustworthy way. For over a decade, we’ve built blockchain infrastructure that tokenizes, custodies, trades and settles assets for the world’s leading financial institutions, like PayPal, Venmo, Mastercard and Interactive Brokers.



ABOUT THE TEAM

Our Finance & Accounting team owns the integrity of Paxos’ financial records and provides the insight and controls that let us scale a regulated, multi-entity financial services business with confidence. We partner closely with Treasury, Product, Engineering, Operations, Legal, Compliance and Commercial teams to ensure our financial infrastructure, data and reporting keep pace with a platform that is constantly shipping and expanding into new products and markets.



ABOUT THE ROLE

We are looking for a Controller to own the integrity of Paxos’ financial records and build the accounting infrastructure to support a fast-scaling, regulated financial services business. Reporting to the CFO, you will lead the close process, own our external audit, set accounting policy, and serve as a financial architecture partner to our Product and Engineering teams as they build and evolve the platform.

This is not a maintenance role. Paxos moves fast, ships constantly, and operates across multiple regulated entities. You will be in the room when new products are designed, new fund flows are drawn up, and we expand to new regions. Your job is to keep the accounting function close to the business, make sure it can keep pace, and ensure finance is never a bottleneck or a blind spot.



WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Own the close and financial statements. Drive the monthly, quarterly and annual close end-to-end for all Paxos entities, ensuring timely, accurate financials with zero tolerance for material misstatements. Prepare and review financial statements in accordance with US GAAP and, where required, local reporting requirements.

 - Set and defend accounting policy. Lead technical accounting research for new products, transactions and structures; conclude and document positions; and translate complex conclusions into clear policies, processes and system requirements. Stay ahead of regulatory developments at FASB, SEC and OCC that affect how Paxos accounts for its business.

 - Own financial audit and controls. Serve as the primary point of contact for external auditors from planning through final sign-off. Design, implement and maintain a strong internal controls framework that is fit for an OCC‑regulated trust company and support regulatory examinations and inquiries.

 - Be a financial architecture partner for the business. Work with Product and Engineering at the design phase of new products, features and fund flows to ensure accounting treatment is understood, the platform can support it, and data flows enable accurate and timely financial reporting.

 - Shape quote-to-cash and revenue recognition. Partner with commercial and legal teams to design scalable quote‑to‑cash processes and ensure commercial contracts support the desired revenue recognition outcomes.

 - Build the team and infrastructure. Manage and develop a team of accounting professionals, hiring ahead of the need. Drive automation and process improvement so close, reconciliation and reporting scale with the business without proportional headcount growth.


ABOUT YOU

 - CPA required.

 - 10+ years of progressive accounting experience, ideally including time in fast-scaling or complex, multi-entity environments.

 - Deep US GAAP technical accounting knowledge with a demonstrated ability to research, conclude and document complex issues.

 - Proven ability to partner directly with Product and Engineering teams on financial system desi

source_jobs.title: Manager, Network Operations & Strategy
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT PAXOS

Today’s financial infrastructure is archaic, expensive, inefficient and risky — supporting a system that leaves out more people than it lets in. So we’re rebuilding it.

We’re on a mission to open the world’s financial system to everyone by enabling the instant movement of any asset, any time, in a trustworthy way. For over a decade, we’ve built blockchain infrastructure that tokenizes, custodies, trades and settles assets for the world’s leading financial institutions, like Mastercard, Visa, Robinhood, and PayPal.




THE OPPORTUNITY

We're hiring a Manager, Network Operations & Strategy to drive strategic clarity and operational excellence within the Global Dollar Network (GDN) internally and externally. This role sits at the intersection of strategy, execution, and cross-functional alignment—owning high-leverage initiatives that directly shape how GDN continuously grows and evolves.

In this role, you'll own strategic programs end to end: from defining the right problems to setting clear KPIs, to pursuing and building the plans to realize the opportunities. This is a role for someone who thrives in ambiguity, can structure complex problems into clear plans, and earns trust through sharp thinking and consistent, high-quality delivery.

Success for this role will position you as a leader who shapes company-wide strategy and execution on behalf of the Global Dollar Network.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

Strategic Initiatives & Execution

 - Propose, own, and define key strategic GDN initiatives and programs with minimal guidance from framing through delivery (incl. engagement with legal, finance, business development, external partners, etc.)

 - Proactively identify areas of opportunity, assess their value, and build comprehensive execution plans with clear success criteria, partnering with growth and product teams to understand GDN partner needs and how to realize them

 - Define measurable success criteria/KPIs and drive cross-functional achievement of the plans to realize them based on data, market feedback, and observed progress

Operational Excellence

 - Continuously evaluate our operational processes or information sharing and improve or replace them via AI-enabled workflows, increasing executional velocity and internal collaboration

Go-to-Market & Strategic Planning

 - Proactively identify and weave business, legal, regulatory, and operational considerations into GDN initiative plans before launch and ensure external and internal partner alignment prior to roll-out

 - Partner with the Head of Network Operations and cross-functional leaders (Growth, Product, Finance) to craft and align on KPIs/multi-quarter strategy, and define new investment priorities for the GDN budget

 - Iterate on ROI framework for GDN investments to drive maximum KPI impact

 - Track market context and competitive dynamics to inform strategic direction and identify emerging areas of investment

Cross-Functional Leadership

 - Maintain strong working relationships and earn trust across Account Management, Business Development, Finance, Legal, Ops, and Product teams; agitate constructively for change when needed to drive business outcomes.

 - Communicate program status, strategy, timing, and risks clearly to all stakeholders - including the Exec Team - ensuring no surprises and proactive alignment.

 - Synthesize complex decisions or opportunities requiring input into crisp, actionable narratives that motivate, orient and create alignment across functional partners




ABOUT YOU

 - 5–10 years of experience in product management, management consulting, product or business operations, partner management and/or a related function; Digital assets experience is highly preferred, although not required

 - Proven track record of independently owning and delivering complex, cross-functional strategic initiatives

 - Strong analytical instincts: able to define success metrics, instrument programs for data-driven decision-making, and iterate based o

source_jobs.title: Head of Communications
source_jobs.description:
About Paxos

Today’s financial infrastructure is archaic, expensive, inefficient and risky — supporting a system that leaves out more people than it lets in. So we’re rebuilding it.

We’re on a mission to open the world’s financial system to everyone by enabling the instant movement of any asset, any time, in a trustworthy way. For over a decade, we’ve built blockchain infrastructure that tokenizes, custodies, trades and settles assets for the world’s leading financial institutions, like PayPal, Venmo, Mastercard and Interactive Brokers.

About the role

The Head of Communications will build and lead Paxos’ communications strategy across executive, corporate, policy, internal and product communications. This leader will own how Paxos shows up with the media, regulators, partners, and employees, re-establishing our presence in key conversations while supporting a highly regulated, global business.

What you’ll do

 - Define and execute a long-term, global communications strategy that integrates executive, corporate, policy, internal, and product narratives into a coherent story for Paxos.

 - Lead media and press relations, including proactive storytelling and responsive reputation management, to return Paxos to a strong presence in key financial and crypto outlets.

 - Own company-wide internal and executive communications in partnership with existing internal comms and marketing, especially around high-stakes, regulated topics and major company milestones.

 - Serve as a trusted advisor and thought partner to the CEO & the executive team, shaping narratives, recommendations, and decision-ready options on communications strategy and execution.

 - Use AI tools and systems to increase leverage in content development, research, and measurement

 - Direct and coordinate external agencies, advisors, and cross-functional partners to deliver high-quality, on-message communications across regions and time zones.

About you

 - 12+ years in communications with experience in senior leadership roles, including meaningful time in tech, fintech, or financial services; experience in crypto and blockchain preferred but not required.

 - Deep experience across multiple comms sub-functions (executive, corporate, policy/regulatory, internal, product), with clear depth in at least one and comfort overseeing all.

 - Proven ability to manage press and media relations and to define and execute plans that re-establish a company’s brand in key media and industry forums.

 - Track record of partnering closely with C-level leaders as a trusted extension of their voice.

 - Experience at fintech companies or banks, ideally with the last several years at a crypto-native or crypto-forward company (e.g., built brand and mindshare in crypto/fintech).

 - Strong, existing relationships or demonstrable ability to build relationships with relevant finance/crypto reporters and outlets; ability to operate across US and international markets.

 - Confident, extroverted, humble personality with strong discussion and recommendation skills.

Nice to Have qualifications:

 - Direct experience in crypto-native companies (e.g., Coinbase, Circle) where you helped build a strong brand and media footprint.

 - Prior work closely tied to regulatory or policy communications, especially in Washington DC or other major regulatory hubs.

 - Experience building or significantly scaling a comms function, including hiring and managing internal teams and agencies.

 - Background in payments, fintech, or TradFi with strong connectivity into finance reporters and publications that cover both finance and crypto beats.

Important Notice for Paxos Applicants

We’ve become aware of fraudulent accounts posting as Paxos recruiters on LinkedIn and other platforms. These scammers attempt to deceive applicants into paying for job opportunities or providing personal financial information.

To verify a legitimate Paxos recruiter:

 - We only use @paxos.com email addresses

 - We never ask fo
